title: Gin’s Gratitude Reveals Sanji’s Kindness
description: On pages 24–25, Sanji feeds Gin despite the danger, revealing that his compassion for the hungry matters more to him than restaurant rules or pirate labels.
scene: Gin arrives broken and starving, while the Baratie cooks judge him as an enemy; Sanji alone treats hunger as something that must be answered.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So that cook saved your life,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And you were able to come back here...

dialogue.speaker: Gin
dialogue.text: B-But I never could've imagined that our mother ship would be in this condition...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Allow me to guide you sir,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To baratie, the restaurant on the seas!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Even though I can do the moon- walk and the chakram.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Pheww... the dance world is tough.

summary: This part reveals the core of Sanji’s morality: no matter who a person is, he cannot ignore someone starving in front of him.
